en.extract:
Taczanowski's brushfinch (Atlapetes taczanowskii) is a species of passerine bird in the New World sparrow family Passerellidae. It is found in montane forest and woodland of central Peru. It was formerly considered to be a subspecies of the slaty brushfinch (Atlapetes schistaceus).


Taxonomy
Taczanowski's brushfinch was formally described in 1874 by the Polish zoologist Władysław Taczanowski under the binomial name Buarremon mystacalis based on a specimen collected in the Andes of central Peru. Unfortunately, he chose a specific epithet that had been used in 1852 by Philip Sclater for Arremon mysticalis, now considered to be a junior synonym of Tanagra albofrenatus Boissonneu, 1840, the moustached brushfinch. In 1875 the English ornithologists Philip Sclater and Osbert Salvin proposed the replacement name Buarremon taczanowskii for this species. Taczanowski's brushfinch was formerly treated as a subspecies of the slaty brushfinch (Atlapetes schistaceus). It is now considered as a separate species based on differences in morphology, vocalizations and mitochondrial DNA sequences. The species is monotypic: no subspecies are recognised.


References

'''Taczanowski's brushfinch''' ('''''Atlapetes taczanowskii''''') is a species of [[passerine]] bird in the New World sparrow family [[Passerellidae]]. It is found in montane forest and woodland of central Peru. It was formerly considered to be a [[subspecies]] of the [[slaty brushfinch]] (''Atlapetes schistaceus'').

==Taxonomy==
Taczanowski's brushfinch was [[species description|formally described]] in 1874 by the Polish zoologist [[Władysław Taczanowski]] under the [[binomial name]] ''Buarremon mystacalis'' based on a specimen collected in the Andes of central Peru.<ref>{{ cite journal | last=Taczanowski | first=Władysław | author-link=Władysław Taczanowski | year=1874 | title=Liste des oiseaux recueillis par M. Constatin Jelski dans la partie centrale du Pérou occidental | language=French | journal=Proceedings of the Zoological Society of London | pages=501-565 [515] | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/28502690 }}</ref> Unfortunately, he chose a specific epithet that had been used in 1852 by [[Philip Sclater]] for ''Arremon mysticalis'', now considered to be a [[junior synonym]] of ''Tanagra albofrenatus'' [[Auguste Boissonneau|Boissonneu]], 1840, the [[moustached brushfinch]].<ref>{{ cite journal | last=Sclater | first=Philip | author-link=Philip Sclater | year=1852 | title=Description de six oiseaux appartenant à la collection du Muséum d'histoire naturelle de Paris | language=French | journal=Revue et Magasin de Zoologie Pure et Appliquée | series=2nd series | volume=4 | pages=8-9 [8] | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/2318737 }}</ref><ref>{{ cite book | editor-last=Paynter | editor-first=Raymond A. Jr | year=1970 | title=Check-List of Birds of the World | volume=13 | publisher=Museum of Comparative Zoology | place=Cambridge, Massachusetts | page=196 | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/14483431 }}</ref> In 1875 the English ornithologists [[Philip Sclater]] and [[Osbert Salvin]] proposed the replacement name ''Buarremon taczanowskii'' for this species.<ref>{{ cite journal | last1=Sclater | first1=Philip Lutley | author1-link=Philip Sclater | last2=Salvin | first2=Osbert | author2-link=Osbert Salvin  | year=1875 | title=On Venezuelan birds collected by Mr. A. Goering. Part V  | journal=Proceedings of the Zoological Society of London | pages=234-238 [236, Plate 35, fig. 2] | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/28501447 }}</ref> Taczanowski's brushfinch was formerly treated as a [[subspecies]] of the [[slaty brushfinch]] (''Atlapetes schistaceus''). It is now considered as a separate species based on differences in morphology, vocalizations and mitochondrial DNA sequences.<ref>{{Cite journal | last1=Sánchez-González | first1=L.A. | last2=Navarro-Sigüenza | first2=A.G. | last3=Krabbe | first3=N.K. | last4=Fjeldså | first4=J. | last5=García-Moreno | first5=J. | date=2015 | title=Diversification in the Andes: the ''Atlapetes'' brush-finches | journal=Zoologica Scripta | volume=44 | issue=2 | pages=135–152 | doi=10.1111/zsc.12088}}</ref><ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | editor3-last=Rasmussen | editor3-first=Pamela | editor3-link=Pamela C. Rasmussen | date=July 2024 | title=New World Sparrows, Bush Tanagers | work=IOC World Bird List Version 14.2 | url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/sparrows/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union | access-date=31 October 2024}}</ref> The species is [[monotypic]]: no [[subspecies]] are recognised.<ref name=ioc/>
